title: Leochilus cochlearis
taxonomy: Leochilus cochlearis is a species of orchid, specifically classified within the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Leochilus, which is a small group of orchids native to Central and South America. Leochilus cochlearis is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae, one of the largest subfamilies of orchids. This classification places it among the terrestrial orchids, which derive nutrients from the surrounding soil rather than relying on epiphytic growth. The classification of Leochilus cochlearis is still subject to ongoing research and refinement in the field of botany.

intro: Leochilus cochlearis, also known as the "Pigille Orchid," is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Leochilus cochlearis include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various infections and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
